title: What Does This Ceasefire Actually Mean? || Peter Zeihan

transcript.text: Hey everybody, Peter Zeihan here coming
to you from a business center in an
exciting hotel because my room's not
ready and it's storming outside. Anyway,
uh today we're going to talk about the
ceasefire that Donald Trump has agreed
to with the Iranians, which is getting
stranger by the day. Now, um
before
the war [clears throat] and when the
Trump administration was originally
negotiating for the ceasefire, there
were three big conditions that the US
has had for the last 40 years.
Number one,
no missile program that's long-range.
Number two, no uranium enrichment, so
you can't even consider getting a
nuclear weapon. And number three, an end
to support for the various sectarian
militant groups throughout the Middle
East. Those have always been America's
three non-negotiable items for the last
many administrations. Uh
the Iranians had their own list, which
was we get to control the Strait of
Hormuz. We get to keep our missiles. We
get to have do enrichment. Uh the axis
of resistance, that's all those
sectarian groups, you can't touch those.
Those are ours. And on and on and on and
on. Now,
24 hours before Donald Trump's
self-appointed deadline, which was at
8:00 p.m. Tuesday,
24 hours before that, the Trump
administration, Donald Trump personally
rejected all of the Iranian conditions
reflexively, which, you know, makes
sense. Um
as he was accepting the ceasefire,
however, on Tuesday night, he said,
"Yeah, these are these are these are
these are things we can talk about." Um
>> [laughter]
>> Uh let me start by saying that Donald
Trump has said a lot of really stupid
over the course of the last couple
of hundred hours. Uh and I'm hoping that
this is just the latest in the whole
line of crazy that has brought us to
this point in the conflict.
>> [snorts]
>> Uh but if he actually means to give away
on any of these, it would be the
greatest capitulation of American power
in the Middle East in the history of the
public. On top of what has already been
the greatest voluntary withdrawal of
power
uh in American history with repudiation
of the alliances that is going on. Um
Trump will make the history books,
that's for sure. Now, we're early days.
We won't even have our first
face-to-face meeting between Trump
administration people and Iranians until
Friday in Pakistan. So, you know, all
those negotiations been hearing about,
they're at best through third parties.
We'll finally actually see them meeting
face-to-face. But uh the Iranians are
proceeding on their understanding of the
ceasefire, which is their point 10
points are fine. So, they have upped
their oil exports. They have maintained
control of the Strait of Hormuz. They
are not letting anyone through, even now
under the ceasefire, unless these people
apply, declare their cargo, pay a bribe
of about a dollar per barrel or 2
million dollars per ship, roughly, and
then get partially escorted through. The
Iranians have already enforced that in
the ceasefire environment on April 8th.
Uh
>> [snorts]
>> Trump said very clearly that the
ceasefire means that the Strait of
Hormuz is open and free for everybody,
and that is absolutely not the case. The
Iranians are absolutely impressing their
view on how the area works. Um if we
stick with this, uh Donald Trump is
going to look
even dumber than he does, and it's
something that has already set the
MAGAsphere on fire. Uh none other than
Laura Loomer, who is
Oh god, she's such a horrid human being.
Um she is the person who convinced Trump
to fire everybody in the National
Security Council who knew what they were
doing because they were insufficiently
loyal. She has even called out Donald
Trump for doing the stupidest thing in
American foreign policy history, her
words, uh and that Iran has now had the
greatest victory uh of the last 40
years, her words. We're seeing
unraveling in the White House right now.
Trump uh has either lost his mind or
he's gotten so bored of the situation
he's willing to do anything to move on.
Uh Friday is going to be a wild
ride as whatever the delusion he's
having at the moment hits the reality of
what's actually happening in an Iran.
And I would love love love to have a
forecast as to how that's going to go,
but everything is so random in the White
House, I really have no idea. So, stay
tuned. I don't know [snorts] what it's
going to be, but it's certainly not
going to be boring.
